Japan Team

  • Rob Roggema and Wanglin Yan, 2017, Tsunami and Fukushima Disaster: Design for Reconstruction. Springer.
    W. Yan and W. Galloway. Rethinking Resilience: Adaptation and Transformation, Springer: The Netherlands, 400p, 2017.3.19.
    W. Yan.  Interfaces of Science and Policy for Climate Change Adaptation and Disaster Risk Reduction., In P. Sawhney & M. A. Perkins (eds.) Emerging Climate Change Adaptation Issues in The Asia-Pacific Region. Institute of Global Environmental Strategy (IGES), pp. 138–162, 2015.
  • Wang, C., Zhang, S., Yan, W., Wang, R., Liu, J., & Wang, Y. (2016). Evaluating renewable natural resources flow and net primary productivity with a GIS-Emergy approach: A case study of Hokkaido, Japan. Nature Scientific Report, 6, 2016.11.18. doi:10.1038/srep37552
  • Shaw R., Izumi T. and Shi P. (2016): Perspectives of Science and Technology in Disaster Risk Reduction of Asia, in International Journal of Disaster Risk Science, 7, 329-342.
    Parvin G. A., Shimi A. C., Shaw R. and Biswas C. (2016): Flood in a changing climate: the impact on livelihood and how the rural poor cope in Bangladesh, in Climate, 4, 60; doi:10.3390/cli4040060
    Banba M. and Shaw R. (2016): Land use management in disaster risk reduction, Springer, 473 pages.
    Shaw R., Rahman A., Surjan A. and Parvin G. (2016): Urban Risk Reduction: Asian perspective, Elsevier Publisher, 370 pages.
    Shiwaku K., Sakurai A. and Shaw R. (2016): Disaster resilience of education systems: Experiences from Japan, Springer, 241 pages.

Qatar Team

  • Grichting, A. (2017). A productive permaculture campus in the desert: visions for Qatar University. Future of Food: Journal on Food, Agriculture and Society, 5(1), 21-33. http://futureoffoodjournal.org/index.php/journal/article/view/261
  • Grichting, A., Awwaad, R., Eribi, N., Mohannadi, A. A. (2016). Designing For Food Security In A Dryland Metropolis. Investigating Productive Landscapes In Doha. Acta Horticulturae, 1108 , 165-172. http://www.actahort.org/books/1108/1108_21.htm
  • Grichting, A. (2015). Boundaryscapes: A Digital And Dynamic Atlas For Collaborative Planning In Cyprus. Territorio. Rivista del DAStU – Dipartimento di Architettura e Studi Urbani, Politecnico di Milano, Franco Angeli Editore, 72 , 108-116.

USA Team

  • Thün, G., Velikov, K., Zielinski, S. (2016) ACCESS-ENABLING ARCHITECTURES: New Hybrid Multi-modal Spatial Prototypes Towards Resource and Social Sustainability, NEXTRANS Project No. 163UMY2.2: USDOT Region V Regional University Transportation Center Final Report
  • Thün, G., Velikov, K., Ripley, C., McTavish, D, (2015) Infra-Eco-Logi Urbanism: A Project for the Great Lakes Megaregion. Zurich, CH: Park Books, 2015 ISBN: 978-3-906027722
  • Thün, G., Velikov, K., (2014) “CONDUIT Urbanism: Regional Ecologies of Energy and Mobility,” inA22 New Ecologies for Osmotic Infrastructures, eds. Pino Scaglione and Mosè Ricci (New York, List / Actar) : 306-327
  • Thün, G., Velikov, K., (2012) “Conduit Urbanism: Rethinking Infrastructural Ecologies in the Great Lakes Megaregion, North America,” in Sustainable Energy Landscapes, eds. Sven Stremke and Andy van Dobbelsteen (London: CRC Press/ Taylor & Francis): 261-284.

Netherlands Team

  • Albers R.A.W., Bosch P.R., Blocken B., Dobbelsteen A.A.J.F. van den et al.; ‘Overview of challenges and achievements in the climate adaptation of cities and in the Climate Proof Cities program’, in: Building and Environment 83, 2015 (1-10)
  • Dobbelsteen A. van den, Broersma S. & Stremke S.; ‘Energy Potential Mapping for energy-producing neighbourhoods’, in: International Journal of Sustainable Building Technology and Urban Development, Vol. 2, No. 2, 2011 (170-176).

UK Team

  • G.P. Keeffe,  Hardware Software Interface: a Strategy for the implementation of Urban Agriculture. Sustainable Urban Agriculture and Food Planning. Roggema, R. (ed.). London: Routledge, Taylor & Francis Group, 20 p. 29 Jul 2016.
  • G.P.Keeffe, Productive Synergies in the City. Why we need small cows: ways to design for urban agriculture. Roggema, R. & Keeffe, G. (eds.). Wageningen: VHL University Press, p. 75-97 23 p. 14 Jan 2015
  • G.P. Keeffe and McHugh I.  ‘IDEAHaus: a modular approach to climate resilient UK Housing.’ Buildings Special Edition Low Carbon Housing Design. Buildings 2014, 4(4)
  • G.P.Keeffe Biproductive urban landscapes: new options for the collapsoscape. In Stremke and vd DobblesteenSustainable Energy LandscapesTaylor and Francis 2012.
  • G.P.Keeffe and McHugh I., Climate Adaptation: The future of extra-care housing for the elderly in the United Kingdom.SB11, Helsinki October 2011.
